dialogues: Here's an array: 3 rows of 8. That's 24 dots. Now look at these four arrays. They ALL have 24 dots. But which one is the SAME array as 3 × 8, just rotated?

Right. 8 rows of 3. That's 3 rows of 8, just turned. Same dots, same product. Now look at the others: 4 × 6, 6 × 4, 2 × 12. They all have 24 dots too, but they're different shapes. You can't rotate 3 rows of 8 and get 4 rows of 6. Same product doesn't mean same array.
